display.purpose: Hero trip titles over the dark film still; light weight, obsession word steps to 500.

headline.purpose: Philosophy and waitlist headings; light, airy, generous.

title.purpose: Per-trip detail title; obsession word steps to weight 400 in ink (no color).

label.purpose: Tracked-uppercase eyebrows, field labels, meta, nav, button text. The luxury signal; often paired with a short ink rule.

shadows.name: print-frame
shadows.purpose: Hairline frame around photographs. The only 'elevation'; the system is otherwise flat.

motion.name: ease-quiet
motion.purpose: House ease for reveals, hover, and the hero cross-fade. Slow and elegant.

motion.name: hero-crossfade
motion.purpose: Slow trip image swap behind the quiet index.

motion.name: hero-push
motion.purpose: Very slow 1.06x scale push on the active hero image.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Slow scroll-in reveal. Visible by default; JS-gated entrance with a transition-free safety net.

components.name: Quiet Index Title
components.description: Signature hero list item: refined light-weight trip title (obsession word stepped up in weight), tracked tabular date kept on the same line. Over the dark film still. The 'freeks' wordmark above it is terracotta.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid ink, square, tracked-caps. Hover lifts the fill to terracotta with white text (~4.8:1).

components.name: Tracked Label
components.description: The luxury signal: tracked-uppercase label, optionally preceded by a short ink rule.

components.name: Interest Chip
components.description: Square, tracked-caps interest toggle. Active fills ink, not the accent.

components.name: Underline Field
components.description: Boxless field flat on the ground: ink text over a bottom rule, tracked-caps label above. Focus rule turns terracotta.

narrative.overview: freeks reads like an expensive, understated travel house, an Aman that happens to be for obsessives. You enter through a dark film still (the hero): a full-bleed, dimmed photograph with the trip list over it in a refined light-weight sans. Then the page opens into a calm, daylit editorial body on warm bone and stone, with enormous whitespace, hairline rules, and tracked-uppercase labels, and closes on a dark footer that bookends the hero. The only chroma is a single terracotta, used as the brand-name color and the interface accent. Hierarchy comes from scale, weight, and space within one typeface (Switzer). Luxury here is the absence of noise.
keyCharacteristics: Cinematic dark film-still hero, light editorial body, dark footer (bookended dark)
Warm bone + stone grounds; deep restraint and enormous whitespace
One refined sans (Switzer) across weights; light display, medium tracked-caps labels
A single terracotta (#b8542a): both the brand name and the interface accent
Square corners, hairline rules, flat surfaces, slow elegant motion
Specificity and tabular numbers as the luxury; no bright color anywhere

rules.name: The One-Terracotta Rule
rules.body: Terracotta #b8542a is the only chroma, and it is one color: the brand name 'freeks' everywhere it appears, plus the interface accent (link underlines, field focus, focus outlines, button-hover). Tuned to read on both the dark hero/footer and the light bone/stone. No second accent hue.

rules.name: The No-Bright-Surface Rule
rules.body: No surface is ever saturated or bright: no colored rooms, no gradients, no glass. Every ground is bone, stone, or near-black. Color lives only in the photography and the single terracotta.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Color Rule
rules.body: Title emphasis (the obsession word) comes from a step up in weight, never from color or a size jump. The label rule before eyebrows is ink, not terracotta.

rules.name: The Tabular-Truth Rule
rules.body: All comparable numbers (benchmarks, distances, times, dates, prices) use tabular figures. The data is the proof of seriousness; it aligns.

rules.name: The Hairline Rule
rules.body: Separation is a 1px rule or a change of ground, never a shadow or a box. Photographs get a 1px inset frame; sections divide by ground and whitespace.

dos: Do bookend the page dark (hero, footer) around a light bone/stone editorial body.
Do keep terracotta as the one warm color: the brand name plus a rationed interface accent.
Do carry hierarchy with weight, scale, and whitespace within one typeface (Switzer).
Do use tracked-uppercase labels with a short ink rule where they carry information.
Do keep surfaces flat and corners square; separate with hairlines and space.
Do set every comparable number in tabular figures, and keep hero title + date on one line.
Do let cinematic, full-color photography carry the body; the dark hero film still sets the mood.
donts: Don't introduce any second color, bright accent, gradient mesh, or glassmorphism.
Don't let terracotta become a fill or a surface (button-hover is the one brief exception); it is never body text.
Don't add a second typeface or a third ground color.
Don't round corners or add shadows, panels, or cards.
Don't build a generic travel-operator layout or drift into hustle-retreat wellness, SaaS scaffolding, or influencer-luxury gloss.
Don't use em dashes in copy; en dashes for numeric ranges only.
Don't crowd the page; whitespace is the luxury. When in doubt, add space and remove elements.
